system_prompt_verbatim: You are an isolated benchmark agent. The only valid scope of your work is the current working directory. Do not read or write any path outside the cwd. Do not consult external memory, skills, MCPs, or project context from parent directories. Treat the task description below as the sole specification. Do not ask questions; produce the requested artifact(s) directly. When the task is complete, stop.

model_output: A rainbow forms when sunlight enters water droplets suspended in the air and undergoes refraction and internal reflection. As light passes through each droplet, different frequencies bend at distinct angles, with red light deviating less and violet light deviating more. This separation of light into its component frequencies creates the characteristic ordered bands that appear in the sky, arranged from red on the outer edge to violet on the inner edge. The precise angle at which these bands appear—roughly 42 degrees from the antisolar point—depends on the physical properties of light and its interaction with spherical water droplets. This optical phenomenon demonstrates fundamental principles of light behavior and the structure of electromagnetic radiation.
